metadata.notes: opencode loads AGENTS.md + emits in-band text (agent=partial, model-dependent on the recorded local Gemma); the marker scan is now ported to the opencode adapter so the daemon surfaces task_estimate + task_completion_eta from its custom SQLite path (daemon=full, no longer a bug); recipe still needs an instruction-file seed step the driver lacks (gap:seed_instruction) -> route driver-gap.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

- **agent = partial** (UNCHANGED). The scenario is agent-agnostic: any agent that (a) loads a persistent instruction file and (b) emits the marker in transcript-visible assistant text can satisfy it. opencode does both. The binary references `AGENTS.md`/`CLAUDE.md` and ships an `"instructions": ["AGENTS.md", …]` config key — opencode loads a global `~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md` and a project-level `AGENTS.md` as its persistent rule file (the AGENTS.md-equivalent of CLAUDE.md). opencode's assistant `text` parts are plain transcript-visible prose, so a model that follows the rule would emit the hidden HTML-comment marker there. The downgrade from `yes` is the RECORDED local setup, not the architecture: the committed `~/.config/opencode/opencode.json` defaults to `lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored` (a ~2–4B local model used in every prior opencode recording). Reliably FOLLOWING an "emit a JSON-in-HTML-comment progress marker and increment it every turn" rule is doubtful for a model that small — the 2026-05-31 marker experiment found 0% exact-format compliance even from Claude (the daemon parser is deliberately tolerant of key/format drift for exactly this reason). So the capability is structurally present but emission is model-dependent and unconfirmed → `partial`; only a live recording can promote it. (A capable cloud model is now also configured under the `ionos` provider — e.g. Qwen3-Coder-Next 80B — which could be selected per-turn via the driver's `-m` model field if the local model proves incapable; that's a recording knob, not an agent-capability change.)

- **daemon = full** (was `bug`; FLIPPED by commit 1ae457d9). The per-adapter content scan that was missing is now present in the opencode adapter. `scanTaskEstimate` was promoted to the shared `tailer.ScanTaskEstimate` (`core/pkg/tailer/taskestimate_scan.go`) — adapter-agnostic, like `ExtractAssistantText` — because the marker contract is plain text. Two opencode wires consume it: (1) `opencode/parser.go parseTextPart` now scans the FULL part text via `tailer.ScanTaskEstimate(text, ev.Timestamp)` and sets `ev.TaskEstimate` BEFORE the 200-rune display truncation, so early markers in long messages survive (parser.go:206-210); (2) crucially, opencode does NOT flow metrics through the tailer — it uses `ProcessOwnedStore` + `ComputeMetrics` — so `querySessionMetrics` (the tailer-bypassing SQLite path) now accumulates the latest estimate (`lastTaskEstimate`, latest-wins) over the full `part.data` text and mirrors the shared `metrics/adapter.go` domain conversion: it sets `metrics.TaskEstimate = &session.TaskEstimate{…}` and projects `metrics.TaskCompletionEta` via `session.ForecastTaskCompletion` (metrics.go:232-276). The shared suppression-once-the-session-leaves-`working` projection is unchanged. So the two spec-required observables (`task_estimate {total_rounds, completed_rounds, updated_at}` + `task_completion_eta` burning down) now surface for opencode from its custom path. Verified in the tree by an end-to-end `TestComputeMetrics_TaskEstimate` over the SQLite harness (latest marker 3/6 wins, `UpdatedAt` from the latest part, projected ETA non-nil) plus parser truncation-trap tests — all green (`go test ./core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/... -race -count=1` → ok). Trace exists + daemon handles it correctly → `daemon=full`.

- **driver = gap:seed_instruction** (UNCHANGED). Scenario `process` step 1 requires seeding the marker rule into the workspace's `AGENTS.md` (the CLAUDE.md-equivalent persistent instruction file) BEFORE the first send. No driver in the repo has any instruction-file-seeding primitive: the opencode driver's `settings` blob is written ONLY to `RUN_CWD/opencode.json` (driver-interactive.sh:133-139), never to `AGENTS.md`, and there is no `seed_instruction`/`file`/`write` step type in any `driver-*.sh`. The rule TEXT (the fenced marker example) must live in an actual file opencode loads, not in `opencode.json` and not as a prompt (it must be a *persistent* rule, per the scenario). So the recipe needs a `seed_instruction` step the driver lacks → `gap:seed_instruction`. This is tooling work, not an observability limit: `record` ports the step (write the rule to `$RUN_CWD/AGENTS.md`, or add `"AGENTS.md"` to the seeded `opencode.json` `instructions` and write the file) before driving.

## Route

`partial | full | gap:*` → **driver-gap** (the daemon no longer routes this cell; the seed_instruction driver gap is now the sole blocker). `record` (1) ports the `seed_instruction` step, then (2) drives the multi-step task and verifies the (now-CORRECT) spec. The spec is NO LONGER `known_failing` on account of the daemon — the marker scan ships — though emission remains model-dependent (agent=partial); a recording promotes that to settled.
caveats: agent=partial is provisional and model-dependent: the recorded default model (lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored, ~2-4B local) may be incapable of reliably emitting/incrementing the marker; only a live recording confirms emission. A capable cloud model (ionos/Qwen3-Coder-Next 80B) is configured and selectable per-turn via the driver -m field if needed.

daemon=full as of 1ae457d9: the opencode adapter now scans the full part.data text via the shared tailer.ScanTaskEstimate and the SQLite ComputeMetrics path sets metrics.TaskEstimate + projects task_completion_eta (mirroring the shared metrics adapter). Confirmed by TestComputeMetrics_TaskEstimate + parser truncation-trap tests; this is a doc/code-based daemon=full, but the marker EMISSION (an agent property) is still unconfirmed — see agent caveat.

The scan runs over the full part.data text (parseTextPart sets ev.TaskEstimate before the 200-rune AssistantText truncation; the metrics path reads the latest estimate). The earlier truncation trap is closed by design.
driver gap is seed_instruction (write a persistent AGENTS.md rule into the workspace); record ports it before driving. The settings->opencode.json seam does NOT write AGENTS.md.

sources.note: ScanTaskEstimate — the marker content scan promoted to the shared tailer package (adapter-agnostic), consumed by every adapter including opencode
parseTextPart now scans the FULL part text via tailer.ScanTaskEstimate and sets ev.TaskEstimate BEFORE the 200-rune display truncation

querySessionMetrics (the tailer-bypassing SQLite path) accumulates the latest estimate (latest-wins) and sets metrics.TaskEstimate + projects metrics.TaskCompletionEta via session.ForecastTaskCompletion — mirrors the shared metrics adapter
TestComputeMetrics_TaskEstimate — end-to-end over the SQLite harness: latest marker (3/6) wins, UpdatedAt from latest part, projected TaskCompletionEta non-nil; green in tree
parser truncation-trap tests — marker in a >200-rune block is still scanned
feat(adapters): port task-estimate marker scan to codex, pi, aider, opencode (#558) — flips this cell's daemon pillar bug->full
opencode is ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite), routes metrics through ComputeMetrics — bypasses the tailer path, which is why the scan had to be wired into the custom metrics path too
shared projection: TaskEstimate -> ForecastTaskCompletion -> task_completion_eta; opencode's ComputeMetrics now mirrors this
ForecastTaskCompletion — shared ETA projection from estimate + elapsed
settings blob is seeded ONLY as RUN_CWD/opencode.json; no AGENTS.md / instruction-file seed step exists (the remaining gap)
DRIVE_ELICITS step grammar — no seed_instruction/file/write primitive
recorded default model lmstudio/gemma-4-e2b-it-uncensored (~2-4B local); ionos cloud provider with Qwen3-Coder-Next 80B also configured
opencode loads AGENTS.md (global ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md + project-level) as its persistent instruction/rules file; configurable via the instructions key

script.text:
## Task progress marker (irrlicht)

While working a multi-phase task, emit a hidden progress marker in your own reply text on EVERY turn, exactly in this form (keep it as an HTML comment so it stays out of the rendered answer):

```
<!-- {"marker":"irrlicht-eta","total_rounds":N,"completed_rounds":M} -->
```

Set total_rounds to the number of phases you plan. Emit it with completed_rounds=0 on your first reply, then INCREASE completed_rounds by 1 each turn as you finish a phase, until completed_rounds == total_rounds on the final turn.

Plan a 3-phase refactor of a small calculator module: (1) add input validation, (2) extract the arithmetic into a helper, (3) add unit tests. Do ONE phase per reply. On your VERY FIRST reply, before doing phase 1, emit the progress marker with total_rounds=3 and completed_rounds=0, then do phase 1 and update completed_rounds to 1. Remember to emit the marker on every reply.
Good. Now do phase 2 (extract the arithmetic helper) and update the progress marker to completed_rounds=2.
Now do phase 3 (add the unit tests). This is the final phase, so set the progress marker to completed_rounds=3 (== total_rounds) and confirm the task is done.
